title: runtime notify webhook email H2 H3 only
purpose: I5.2 defines runtime notify webhook email h2 h3 only for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I5.2` only; siblings under `I5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: When journal-keeper records an H2 blocker or requests H3 sign-off, the runtime may emit an optional webhook or email per [A6.2](A6.2-notify-digest-on-h2-blocker-not-every-step.md)—routine pursuit steps and phase completions do not trigger outbound notify.
Notify payloads carry goal_id, structured blocker reason, and suggested operator action so recipients can act without opening the full journal.
Delivery runs outside the critical pursuit path; webhook or SMTP failure logs locally and pursuit continues without waiting for operator acknowledgment.
Strict environments may disable outbound notify in state or pack config; operators still observe status via [I5.1](I5.1-runtime-notify-status-dashboard-generation.md) dashboard regeneration.

If an alert was missed, the journal Blockers section and H3 pending state remain authoritative—notify is observability only and never substitutes for [A6.3](A6.3-operator-observe-without-unblocking-loop.md) clearance to resume autopilot.

narrative: I5.2 defines runtime notify webhook email h2 h3 only for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system when journal-keeper records an h2 blocker or requests h3 sign-off, the runtime may emit an optional webhook or email per [a6.2](a6.2-notify-digest-on-h2-blocker-not-every-step.md)—routine pursuit steps and phase completions do not trigger outbound notify, then notify payloads carry goal_id, structured blocker reason, and suggested operator action so recipients can act without opening the full journal.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

Optional webhook and email notifications fire on H2 blocker detection and H3 sign-off events only—not every pursuit step ([A6.2](A6.2-notify-digest-on-h2-blocker-not-every-step.md)). Payloads carry structured blocker reason, goal id, and suggested operator action.

Notification failure must not block pursuit; missed alerts are recoverable from journal Blockers section. Strict environments may disable outbound notify entirely; observation remains via dashboard ([I5.1](I5.1-runtime-notify-status-dashboard-generation.md)).
